details: Explored the PHP app structure (front controller `public/index.php`, routing via `Core\Router`, controllers under `App\Controllers`, views under `App\Views`, models under `App\Models`, and DB schema migrations under `database/`.)
Identified that the original app was domain-specific (PAPeR/GRM) with modules for profiles, structures, grievances, library, options library, and related APIs, plus admin/security/audit scaffolding.

summary.topic: Goal: make the scaffold reusable for other systems
details: User requested removing domain-specific traces/current objectives while keeping the basic admin/development scaffold so it can be reused for other systems.
We investigated RBAC/capabilities (`App\Capabilities`), routing (`public/index.php`), and tenant scoping hooks (`App\UserProjects`).
We replaced `public/index.php` routing to keep only generic admin modules (auth, dashboard, settings/system, users/roles, notifications, and minimal API endpoints).

We created new minimal migrations: `database/migration_000_initial.php` and `database/migration_001_platform.php`, and removed older domain migrations (`migration_001`–`migration_027`) plus domain seeds/CLI/domain modules.

We simplified core scaffold files to match the new generic scope (e.g., `App\Capabilities`, `App\ListConfig`, `App\UserProjects` as a no-op by default, `App\Controllers\UserController`, and scaffold-level `App\Controllers\Api\DashboardController` and `ApiController`).
We simplified/adjusted notification + settings scaffolding to generic terminology and removed module-specific notification preference UI fields.
We updated in-app layout branding/labels (e.g., “Admin App”, “My account”, generic help/admin guide wording) and replaced PAPeR-specific strings.
User asked whether documentation was updated; we checked Markdown docs and in-app help pages.
We updated `DEVELOPMENTGUIDE.md` and `CHANGES.md` to reflect the reusable admin scaffold state (minimal migrations 000/001, extension points, and removal of domain-specific module references).
We also updated in-app help pages: `App\Views\help\index.php` and `App\Views\admin\guide.php` to match the scaffold approach.

summary.topic: New product planning: Church multi-tenant web app
details: User wants a web application for a church organization: many churches with a Central Office.
Confirmed requirements: MVP first (members directory + announcements + service schedule + attendance). Giving/donations later.
Access model: one domain only (same app, no subdomains).
Data sensitivity: strict audit + retention policy required (especially around member personal info and audit records).
Proposed plan: multi-tenant schema using a `church_id` scoped design; Central Office can view/manage across churches, church staff scoped to their assigned church(s).
MVP table suggestions: `churches`, `user_churches`, `members`, `announcements`, `services`, `attendance_sessions`, `attendance_records`—all church-scoped with indexes.
Audit strategy: reuse scaffold `audit_log` pattern; ensure key write actions log changes and recommend storing `church_id` in audit for fast filtering.
Retention strategy: implement soft-archive fields (e.g., `archived_at` / locked states) and a planned purge job (cron/CLI) driven by retention years stored in settings.

open_questions_for_tomorrow: For attendance MVP: do you want per-member status only (present/absent) or also groups/roles per service?
Who can edit members in MVP (Church Admin only vs additional staff roles)?
Retention strictness: should audit log be effectively permanent, or is there a target retention duration (e.g., 5/7/10 years) for audit and member PII?
